focus: Decision guidance — path selection, subject derivation, cover format handling

tasks.instruction: Generate a gonzo cover for an article with this abstract: 'Large language models don't reason the way we think they do. They pattern-match across a latent space of training data, assembling responses from statistical proximity rather than logical necessity. This isn't a bug — it's a fundamentally different kind of cognition, one we keep misrecognizing because we evaluate it against human benchmarks.' No reference image available.
tasks.input_data: Core thesis: LLM cognition is pattern-matching, not reasoning. We misrecognize it because we use the wrong benchmarks. No reference.
tasks.expected_outcome: A SUBJECT line that captures the friction point of pattern-matching cognition being mistaken for reasoning. Concrete, friction point present, no style words.
tasks.success_criteria: SUBJECT captures the 'misrecognition' friction. Concrete objects (books, maps, patterns, mirrors). No style words. Template verbatim. 1600px JPEG Q85.

tasks.instruction: You need a gonzo cover for an article titled 'The Quiet Collapse of Attention.' The article is about how modern digital interfaces fragment attention into micro-slices. A reader submitted a photo of their cat sitting on a keyboard as a reference image. The reference has no thematic connection to the article's subject.
tasks.input_data: Reference image exists at assets/portia-full-face-reference.png but it's a spider face, not related to the article. Article is about attention fragmentation in digital interfaces.
tasks.expected_outcome: Agent recognizes the reference image is thematically irrelevant and chooses Path A (text-to-image from scratch) rather than forcing an irrelevant reference into Path B.
tasks.success_criteria: Path A chosen. SUBJECT captures attention fragmentation (screens, threads, mirrors, overlapping timelines, fractured light). Reference image NOT used despite existing. Template verbatim.

tasks.instruction: Audit a post directory at /tmp/test-post/ and handle its cover. The directory has: cover.png (4000x3000, 5MB), frontmatter references 'cover: /images/old-cover.png'. Determine the correct approach: resize existing? regenerate? update frontmatter?
tasks.input_data: Post has cover.png at wrong size (4000x3000). Frontmatter points to /images/old-cover.png (absolute path in static/images/). Article: 'The Persistence of Memory in AI Systems.'
tasks.expected_outcome: Agent recognizes the cover.png is non-standard (wrong size, wrong format reference) and follows the correct workflow: either resize+convert+update-frontmatter with existing gonzo cover, or regenerate. Reports decision rationale.
tasks.success_criteria: Either resize or regenerate. Old cover removed. Frontmatter updated to standard cover.jpg convention. Report explains the decision path.
